message:
perf(func): avoid UNHEX text argument allocations

UnhexFunc previously called `args[0].to_text()` and (when given the
ignore-chars argument) `args[1].to_text()`, both of which allocate a
fresh String per invocation even when the underlying SqliteValue is
already text. With UNHEX commonly running over millions of rows in
hex-decoding queries, that pair of allocations is a measurable
overhead on the per-row hot path.

Switches both to the shared `text_arg(&args[N])` helper, which returns
a `Cow<str>` borrowed from the underlying SmallText/Blob storage when
possible. The first argument's filter pipeline now goes through
`input.as_ref().chars()` so the Cow is consumed without extra
copying. The `args[1]` case still collects into `Vec<char>` (we need
random-access membership tests inside the filter closure) but the
intermediate String allocation is gone.

Pairs the change with a perf-only ignored test
(`perf_unhex_text_args`) following the same shape as the surrounding
microbenchmarks: 300_000 invocations × 7 repeats, separately timing
the plain-input path (`UNHEX('48656C6C6F776F726C64')`) and the
two-arg ignore path (`UNHEX('48-65-6C-6C-6F', '-')`), recording the
best wall-clock elapsed nanoseconds for each. Tagged
`#[ignore = "perf-only benchmark"]` so `cargo test` skips it and only
explicit invocations run it.
